Motion And Notice Of Hearing: Motion And Motion To Dismiss For Failure To State A Claim Upon Which Relief Can Be Granted Rule 12(B)6
Off Calendar Aug-30-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - MARK HOOSHMAND OF THE HOOSHMAND LAW GROUP, TEL. NO. 1-415-318-5709 PLAINTIFF APPEARING BY TELEPHONE;
KARLEEN GRIFFIN, TEL. NO. 1-213-267-4637, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Karleen Griffin's motion to dismiss for failure to state a claim is denied. The motion is based on a federal rule of procedure that does not apply in this court. The analogous state court procedure is a demurrer. Subsequent to the filing of this motion, Ms. Griffin filed a demurrer and thus this motion is not only procedurally improper, it has been superseded by the demurrer. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Reyes Hunter, CSR # 6576 =302/hk

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT KARLEEN GRIFFIN'S Motion To Stay Pending Completion Of Related Case #CGC-15-546624 is granted in part. Absent a further order of the court, Plaintiff may not propound discovery from Defendant which is related to or connected to Plaintiff's pending defamation lawsuit, CGC-15-546624. A broader stay is not necessary because Defendant has not demonstrated that the instant action is affected by the anti-SLAPP motion which is currently on appeal in CGC-15-546624 and Defendant has not shown that a stay of all discovery in this action is required by CCP section 426.15(g). Plaintiff's evidentiary objection to the introduction of the arbitration award is overruled. Order signed in open Court.
